world.title: Dubai schools must offer in-person and distance learning options to all students
world.summary: Dubai schools are required to provide both in-person and distance learning options for all students as part of a phased return to classrooms. Schools must meet specific mandatory requirements and obtain official approval before reopening. This initiative aims to ensure a safe transition back to in-person education while accommodating diverse learning needs amidst ongoing developments in the region.

world.title: What is so special about Iran’s uranium that US, China and Russia all want it?
world.summary: Iran’s enriched uranium stockpile is central to a global power struggle involving the United States, China, and Russia. Each country aims to influence the future of this resource, which has significant geopolitical implications. Meanwhile, Iran maintains that it will not relinquish control over its uranium, emphasizing its importance in the ongoing negotiations and international relations surrounding nuclear capabilities.

world.title: How Trump stunned Netanyahu with 'Israel prohibited' post
world.summary: Donald Trump surprised Benjamin Netanyahu by stating that Israel was “prohibited” from striking Lebanon, which contradicted a US-backed ceasefire permitting self-defense. Following Trump's post, Israel requested clarification from the White House, as reported by Axios. The declaration raised concerns among Israeli officials regarding its implications for military operations and regional stability amidst ongoing tensions in the area.

business.title: RBI announced ‘new rules’ for exchanging old ₹500 & ₹1000 notes? PIB Factcheck says…
business.summary: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) has not announced new rules for exchanging old ₹500 and ₹1000 notes, according to a fact-check by the Press Information Bureau (PIB). Users are advised to refrain from sharing unverified messages and to report any suspicious content to the PIB to prevent misinformation regarding currency exchange practices.

business.title: Bonus issue, stock split, dividend: 9 stocks to turn ex-record date next week, check details
business.summary: Between April 20 and April 24, several companies will go ex-record date for bonus issues, stock splits, and dividends. Investors must purchase shares before these record dates to qualify for dividend payments, bonus shares, or stock splits. Key dates and additional details are available for investors to ensure they do not miss out on these opportunities.

business.title: Gold price in India rallies by ₹5000 in two weeks. Is it a right time to buy gold on Akshaya Tritiya?
business.summary: Gold prices in India have increased by ₹5,000 over the past two weeks, rising from ₹1,49,690 to ₹1,54,605 per 10 grams as of April 18, 2026. This surge follows a decline in crude oil prices due to easing tensions in the US-Iran conflict. The upcoming Akshaya Tritiya may prompt buyers to consider purchasing gold at these rates.

technology.title: NVIDIA Introduces GeForce NOW in India Powered by Blackwell RTX Architecture
technology.summary: NVIDIA has launched early access to GeForce NOW™ in India, utilizing the Blackwell RTX architecture. This initiative highlights the significance of the Indian gaming market. The platform aims to provide gamers in India with enhanced cloud gaming experiences. NVIDIA's move reflects its commitment to expanding its services in emerging markets, catering specifically to local gaming needs and preferences.

technology.title: Galaxy S27 Ultra leak reveals faster storage and next-generation processor
technology.summary: Leaks regarding Samsung's upcoming Galaxy S27 Ultra indicate enhancements in storage and processing capabilities. The device is expected to feature UFS 5.0 storage technology and a new Snapdragon 8 Elite Gen 6 Pro processor. These advancements aim to improve performance and efficiency, positioning the Galaxy S27 Ultra as a competitive flagship in the smartphone market.

technology.title: Tech wrap this week: Vivo launches T5 Pro 5G, ASUS unveils Zenbook laptops and more
technology.summary: Vivo launched the T5 Pro 5G smartphone, while Realme introduced the Narzo 100 Lite 5G in India, featuring a MediaTek Dimensity 6300 processor and a 7000mAh battery. ASUS unveiled new Zenbook laptops, and other brands like OPPO, GoPro, and Xiaomi also announced significant product releases, including Mini LED TVs, marking a notable week in technology advancements.

sports.title: Shreyas Iyer's Boundary Rope Spectacular Draws Epic 200-Word Post From "Grandfather of Fielding" Jonty Rhodes
sports.summary: Shreyas Iyer, captain of Punjab Kings, impressed cricket legend Jonty Rhodes with a remarkable catch to dismiss Hardik Pandya during the IPL 2026 match against Mumbai Indians. Rhodes, known as the "Grandfather of Fielding," praised Iyer's awareness and athleticism, highlighting the significance of the catch in contributing to PBKS's comfortable victory in the match.

sports.title: Man United boss provides update on Rashford’s continuity at Barcelona
sports.summary: Marcus Rashford's future at Barcelona is increasingly uncertain as the summer transfer window approaches. Initially perceived as a straightforward deal, complications have arisen, leading to speculation from both parties. Currently on loan from Manchester United, Rashford's performance in Spain has been mixed, contributing to the ongoing discussions regarding his continuity with the club. Further developments are anticipated.

sports.title: 'I woke up and ... ': LizLaz breaks silence after Virat Kohli 'likes' then unlikes her pic
sports.summary: Virat Kohli recently attracted attention after liking and then unliking a photo posted by influencer LizLaz on Instagram. LizLaz addressed the incident, stating, "I woke up and saw it." Kohli, known for his cricketing skills, has often been in the news for his performances on the field, but this social media interaction has sparked public interest.

health.title: World Liver Day: Care Health Insurance Sounds Alarm on India’s Liver Health
health.summary: India is experiencing a significant but overlooked liver health crisis, particularly highlighted on World Liver Day. The Ministry of Health and Family Welfare reports that Nonalcoholic Fatty Liver Disease affects 9% to 32% of the population, indicating that nearly one in three individuals may be impacted. Care Health Insurance has raised concerns regarding the urgency of addressing this health issue.

health.title: The Role of Fexofenadine-Pseudoephedrine Combinations in Pre-and Post-operative ENT Practice
health.summary: Fexofenadine-pseudoephedrine combinations are increasingly utilized in pre-and post-operative ENT practices. ENT disorders affect all age groups and represent over 20% of healthcare visits globally. This highlights their prevalence and the need for effective management strategies. Understanding the clinico-epidemiological profile of these disorders is essential for improving patient outcomes and addressing the significant public health challenges they present.

health.title: New Blood Test MethylScan Promises Early Detection of Cancer and Organ Disorders
health.summary: Researchers at the University of California, Los Angeles have created MethylScan, an affordable blood test capable of simultaneously detecting various cancers, liver diseases, and organ disorders. The test works by analyzing circulating DNA fragments in the bloodstream, offering a promising method for early diagnosis. MethylScan aims to improve patient outcomes through timely identification of health issues.

science.title: Interstellar Comet 3I/ATLAS transforms near the Sun: Scientists reveal shocking chemical changes
science.summary: Interstellar comet 3I/ATLAS exhibited significant chemical changes as it approached the Sun, providing valuable information about its internal structure. These transformations offer insights into the processes involved in planet formation within distant star systems. Observations were made using the Subaru Telescope, highlighting the comet's unique characteristics and the implications for understanding the composition and evolution of celestial bodies.

science.title: Indian scientists find data ‘fully consistent’ for dwarf galaxies to host black holes
science.source: The Sentinel - of this Land, for its People
science.summary: Indian scientists conducted a study examining dwarf spheroidal galaxies orbiting the Milky Way to determine if they host black holes. The findings revealed data that is “fully consistent with the presence of intermediate-mass black holes.” This research contributes to the understanding of black hole formation and the characteristics of these small galaxies, as stated in an official announcement.

science.title: Scientists observe optical vortices exhibiting superluminal motion in light waves
science.summary: Researchers have observed optical vortices, or "dark points," in light waves that exhibit superluminal motion. This discovery provides the first direct experimental evidence supporting a theoretical prediction made decades ago. The findings suggest that under specific conditions, these vortices can move faster than light, advancing the understanding of light wave behavior and potentially influencing future optical technologies.

entertainment.title: Yash defends Ramayana's international canvas and style: 'We need to make it familiar to a Western audience'
entertainment.summary: Yash, portraying Ravana in Nitesh Tiwari's Ramayana, defended the film's international appeal at CinemaCon. He emphasized the importance of making the story relatable to Western audiences. Yash mentioned his efforts to add depth to Ravana's character, aiming to present him as more human. He believes that understanding the essence of the character is crucial for global viewers.

entertainment.title: On Husband KL Rahul's Birthday, Athiya Shetty Shares Unseen Pics From Wedding
entertainment.summary: On April 15, actress Athiya Shetty celebrated her husband KL Rahul's 34th birthday by sharing unseen wedding photos on Instagram. She expressed her affection in a heartfelt note, referring to him as “my person” and stating her love for him. The post highlighted their relationship and showcased intimate moments from their wedding, marking a special occasion for the couple.

entertainment.title: Vijay Deverakonda joins hands with Hi Nanna director Shouryuv for an ambitious project
entertainment.summary: Vijay Deverakonda has officially announced his collaboration with director Shouryuv, known for the film Hi Nanna. This partnership has been speculated for several months, and the confirmation comes via Deverakonda's social media profiles. Further details about the project, including its title and release date, have not yet been disclosed, but excitement is building within the Telugu cinema community.
